presentation_variants.presenting_complaint: Severe headache with flashing lights and nausea
Throbbing one-sided headache after seeing zig-zag lines
Migraine headache with visual aura and light sensitivity
Pounding headache and visual disturbance starting with aura
Recurring migraine with visual changes and photophobia

history_data.hpi: Patient developed a gradual onset throbbing headache approximately 3 hours ago, preceded by 20 minutes of visual aura consisting of flashing lights and zig-zag lines in the peripheral vision. Pain is located on the right side of the head and is associated with significant nausea, photophobia, and phonophobia. Patient has had similar episodes approximately 2–3 times per month for the past 2 years. No fever, neck stiffness, or focal neurological symptoms.
patient_quotes: I always see flashing lights before the headache starts.
Bright light makes everything so much worse.
I feel nauseated and need to lie down in a dark room.
The pain is throbbing on one side of my head.
I've had these headaches for years now — this is typical for me.
pmh_pool: Migraine with aura — recurrent, 2–3 episodes monthly

social_history.alcohol: Occasional alcohol use, aware it triggers migraines
social_history.exercise: Regular exercise — sometimes triggers migraines if intense
social_history.sleep: Adequate sleep; lack of sleep is a known trigger

core_facts.aura_type: visual — flashing lights and zig-zag lines

neurological: Alert and oriented to person, place, and time

investigations.FBC (Full Blood Count): WBC 7.2 × 10⁹/L, Hb 13.5 g/dL, Platelets 265 × 10⁹/L — all within normal limits. No anaemia or infection.
investigations.Urea & Electrolytes: Na 140 mmol/L, K 4.0 mmol/L, Creatinine 82 µmol/L, Urea 5.1 mmol/L — all normal. Adequate hydration status.
investigations.Blood Glucose: 5.3 mmol/L — normal. Hypoglycaemia excluded as trigger.
investigations.Thyroid Function (TSH/T4): TSH 2.1 mIU/L, Free T4 15 pmol/L — normal. Hyperthyroidism excluded.

essential_questions: visual aura — did you see anything unusual before headache?
essential_examinations: assessment for focal deficits or red flags
critical_actions: obtain detailed history of aura and headache characteristics
perform focused neurological examination to exclude red flags
assess for red flag features — if absent, imaging not required for typical migraine
provide acute treatment — triptan or NSAID
offer supportive measures — dark room, rest, fluids
consider preventive therapy if frequent episodes
red_flags: Headache with fever and neck stiffness
management_actions: Acute treatment: Sumatriptan 50mg or NSAID (ibuprofen 400–600mg, naproxen 500–750mg)
Antiemetic if nausea present — metoclopramide or domperidone
Supportive measures: rest in dark, quiet environment, hydration, ice pack
Avoid light and noise triggers during episode
Prophylactic therapy for frequent migraines (>4 per month): Propranolol, Topiramate, or Amitriptyline
Lifestyle modification: identify and avoid triggers (stress, lack of sleep, certain foods, alcohol)
Outpatient neurology referral if migraines worsening or concern about pattern change
Patient education on migraine management and trigger avoidance
important_investigations: CT Head — only if red flags present
critical_history_questions: Was there any warning before the headache — like visual changes or aura?
Describe the aura: what did you see? How long did it last?
Where exactly is the pain? Just one side or both sides?
How severe is the pain on a scale of 0–10?
Are you nauseated? Have you vomited?
Does bright light make it worse?
Have you had headaches like this before?
How often do these headaches happen?
Anyone in your family have migraines?
What usually triggers your migraines — stress, lack of sleep, certain foods, alcohol?
Is this the worst headache of your life? (red flag question)
